A TORNADO RATED AT EF0 WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 2.5 MILES WITH A WIDTH OF ABOUT 50 YARDS. MAINLY TREE DAMAGE. SOME MINOR SIDING DAMAGE TO A HOUSE. PEAK WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 75 MPH. THIS TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND APPROXIMATELY 3 MINUTES.

THIS TORNADO WAS RATED AT EF1 AND WAS ON THE GROUND APPROXIMETLY 2 MINUTES. THE PATH WAS 1.2 MILES LONG AND 75 YARDS WIDE AT TIMES. PEAK WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH. THE PATH WAS FROM 1.5 MILES SOUTH OF ALLENDALE TO 1.5 MILES SOUTHEAST OF ALLENDALE. MAINLY TREE DAMAGE WAS FOUND.
A MICROBURST ABOUT 30 YARDS IN WIDTH AND LESS THAN .5 MILES IN LENGTH OCCURRED 1.5 MILES SOUTHEAST OF ALLENDALE. DAMAGE WAS TO TREES. THIS PATH WAS ABOUT 100 YARDS SOUTH OF THE EF1 TORNADO.

TRAINED SPOTTER/METEOROLOGIST REPORT ESTIMATED 60 MPH THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST AT HIS HOUSE AT 225 PM, OR APPROX 5 MINUTES BEFORE SAME STORM HIT HIS NEARBY NEIGHBORS HOUSE AND TORE OFF 2/3 OF THE ROOF WITH WHAT HE DESCRIBED AS EF0 TYPE WINDS FROM EITHER A TORNADO OR A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST AT 230 PM.

TRAINED SPOTTER/METEOROLOGIST DESCRIBES EF0 LIKE WINDS TORE OFF ABOUT 2/3 OF NEIGHBORS ROOF ABOUT 5 MILES NORTHWEST OF POPLAR BLUFF. SAME STORM HIT HIS OWN HOUSE ABOUT 5 MINS PRIOR, APPROX 225 PM, WITH ESTIMATED 60 MPH WINDS. HE WAS NOT SURE IF IT WAS A TORNADO OR THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST.
